signals.headline: On April 5, 19-year-old rookie **Cooper Flagg** scored 45 points (plus 9 assists, 8 rebounds), leading Dallas Mavericks
signals.raw: On April 5, 19-year-old rookie **Cooper Flagg** scored 45 points (plus 9 assists, 8 rebounds), leading Dallas Mavericks to a 134-128 win over depleted Lakers, snapping a 14-game home losing streak—their longest in 32 years[1][2].
matched_markets: Will Cooper Flagg win the 2025–26 NBA Rookie of the Year award?

signals.headline: This followed his 51-point game, marking back-to-back 40+ point games (first rookie since Allen Iverson), 96 points over
signals.raw: This followed his 51-point game, marking back-to-back 40+ point games (first rookie since Allen Iverson), 96 points over two games (ties Wilt Chamberlain among rookies), and now most 40-point teen games ever (passing LeBron James); he's sixth rookie with 3+ 45-point games (others: Hall of Famers like Michael Jordan)[2].

signals.headline: Impact: Flagg surged in **Rookie of the Year** race, flipping odds to battle Duke teammate Khan Canipple; previously not
signals.raw:
Impact: Flagg surged in **Rookie of the Year** race, flipping odds to battle Duke teammate Khan Canipple; previously not the favorite[2][3]. Lakers (playoff-bound, tied for West 3rd) were without Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves[1].
